threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's normal to carry an umbrella in the sun. Here, it’s always about dodging the rain while the sun somehow beams down.
Last Saturday, saw a lad trying to impress his mates by singing 'Wonderwall' on the 199. The bus driver just shook his head and turned the volume up.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I realized I was truly living here when I saw a kid using a broken skateboard as a makeshift surfboard in the canal. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a bustling market where you can buy fresh falafel and hand-knitted socks in the same aisle. It's a sensory overload!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they avoid eye contact with Muni buses trying to squeeze through 16th and Mission at rush hour. Total pro move.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy in a full tuxedo on the 14 Mission, tying his bow tie and sipping iced coffee like it was totally normal. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's plazas are always buzzing, but try visiting on a rainy Tuesday—it's a ghost town and the locals cherish that quiet.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always spot them at the market, trying to haggle over the price of pa amb tomàquet like it’s a bargain, not a tradition.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 48 bus is just a quick hop to the station, not a designated ‘cross-town adventure’ at rush hour.
Caught a whiff of jerk chicken on the 59 last night. Turns out the guy next to me was bringing dinner home from a pop-up. Smelled like heaven.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Saw a group of kids trading Pokémon cards on a bench while a street performer juggled flaming torches—it clicked, this place is beautifully chaotic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can grab a döner at 2 AM, then find a spontaneous rave in an abandoned factory. Only in Kreuzberg!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local if they grumble about the Muni delay while casually holding a burrito in the other hand, like it’s just another Tuesday.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a saxophonist on the 21 Hayes yesterday, playing 'Take Five' as we stopped at Market. The whole bus started clapping. Pure Muni magic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia's streets are simply charming. They miss the hidden murals and the real stories behind them—like the one about the cat lady on Carrer de Verdi.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still stop to take photos of the street lamps—those quirky modernista designs are lost on most of us locals.
